pytorch.reason: compute det(R) for SO(3) and O(3) matrices; autograd confirms det is preserved under composition; distinguish proper (det=+1) from improper (det=-1) transformations

pyg.reason: not used — chirality bridge is algebraic/matrix level; no graph message-passing required

z3.reason: UNSAT: det=+1 (proper rotation) AND chirality=-1 (flipped) — structural impossibility; SO(3) cannot invert chirality by definition

cvc5.reason: not used — z3 covers the proof layer for this sim

sympy.reason: symbolic verification that pseudoscalar I=e123 commutes with all basis blades in Cl(3,0); confirm I^2=-1 and chirality eigenvalues are exactly +1/-1

clifford.reason: compute pseudoscalar I=e123 in Cl(3,0); verify I commutes with all grade elements; chirality eigenvalue +1 for e_R (even grade) and -1 for e_L (odd grade); rotor double-cover

geomstats.reason: SpecialOrthogonal(n=3) samples verify det=+1 throughout; confirm chirality-preserving property via determinant invariance under SO(3) composition

e3nn.reason: not used — chirality bridge is Clifford algebraic; e3nn equivariant networks not required at this classical baseline level

rustworkx.reason: G-tower DAG annotated with chirality-preserving status: SO(3) preserves chirality, O(3) does not; edge from O(3) to SO(3) marks det constraint

xgi.reason: not used — chirality bridge is matrix algebraic; no hypergraph topology required

toponetx.reason: not used — chirality bridge is algebraic; no cell complex required

gudhi.reason: not used — chirality bridge is algebraic; no persistent homology required

P1_pytorch_so3_det_plus_one.description: SO(3) rotations all have det=+1 — chirality (+1) is preserved under proper rotations

P2_pytorch_reflection_det_minus_one.description: Reflection diag(-1,1,1) has det=-1 — improper transformation inverts chirality to -1

P3_pytorch_so3_composition_preserves_det.description: SO(3) composition R1@R2 preserves det=+1 — chirality is stable under arbitrary rotations

P4_clifford_pseudoscalar_commutes_all.description: Clifford Cl(3,0): pseudoscalar I=e123 commutes with all basis blades — [I,blade]=0 for all grades

P5_clifford_pseudoscalar_squares_minus_o.description: Clifford Cl(3,0): I^2 = -1 — pseudoscalar defines a complex structure on the algebra

P7_sympy_rotation_det_equals_one.description: Sympy: rotation matrix Rz(x) has det=1 symbolically — SO(3) always preserves chirality

P8_geomstats_so3_all_det_plus_one.description: Geomstats SO(3) random samples all have det=+1 — chirality preserved throughout SO(3)

P9_rustworkx_so3_preserves_chirality.description: G-tower DAG: SO(3) node preserves chirality; O(3) node does not — det constraint gates chirality

N1_pytorch_reflection_after_rotation_fli.description: Negative: Refl @ SO(3) gives det=-1 — composition with reflection always flips chirality

N2_z3_proper_rotation_cannot_flip_chiral.description: Z3 UNSAT: det=+1 (proper rotation) AND chirality=-1 (flipped) — SO(3) cannot invert chirality

N3_clifford_rotor_preserves_grade.description: Negative: Clifford rotor (even) sandwich R*e1*R_rev stays grade-1 — no chirality flip from proper rotation

N4_sympy_reflection_det_minus_one.description: Sympy: diag(-1,1,1) has det=-1 — reflections are definitionally chirality-inverting

N5_geomstats_so3_never_negative_det.description: Negative: Geomstats SO(3) samples never yield det<0 — reflections are excluded from SO(3)

N6_rustworkx_so3_connected_to_right_hand.description: Negative: SO(3) node connected only to det=+1 component — no path to det=-1 (left-handed) component

B1_pytorch_rotation_pi_still_det_plus_on.description: Boundary: rotation by pi has det=+1 — even at extreme angle, chirality is preserved

B2_clifford_double_cover_2pi_identity.description: Boundary: Clifford rotor at theta=2pi gives R=-1, but R*e1*R_rev=e1 — double cover; chirality unchanged

B3_sympy_det_at_pi_over_2_equals_one.description: Boundary sympy: det(Rz(pi/2))=1 exactly — chirality unchanged at any specific angle

B4_pytorch_double_reflection_is_rotation.description: Boundary: two reflections composed give det=+1 — double parity flip restores chirality

B5_z3_proper_rotation_consistent_chirali.description: Boundary z3 SAT: det=+1 AND chirality=+1 is consistent — proper rotation preserving chirality has a model

B6_rustworkx_path_to_so3_passes_through_.description: Boundary: shortest path GL→SO(3) always passes through O(3) — orthogonality is prior to chirality-fixing
